examples.title: Distance at Constant Speed
examples.content: d = vt. Speed is constant, so distance proportional to time. 30 m/s: 30m in 1sec, 60m in 2sec. Direct proportionality.

examples.title: Ideal Gas Law
examples.content: PV = nRT. At constant T and n: pressure ∝ 1/volume. Halve volume, double pressure. Inverse proportionality.

examples.title: Brightness and Distance
examples.content: Light spreads out. Brightness ∝ 1/distance². Twice as far, ¼ as bright. Power law inverse.
